question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) wraps the entire Flux including the repeatWhen, meaning a single DB error resets the entire polling loop from the beginning, potentially causing message re-delivery or duplicate processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that context.getPageSize().set(0L) is called at the start of each poll() invocation, and if poll() is called concurrently (e.g., due to scheduler behavior), the pageSize counter could be reset mid-count, causing isComplete() to incorrectly evaluate completion state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with throttled=true and a very small pollingFrequency (if the property is misconfigured to a low value), causing the retriever to hammer the database at high frequency, amplified by the number of concurrent subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled isComplete() check (pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()) where maxPageSize is read from a shared mutable RetrieverProperties bean, and if maxPageSize is changed at runtime, cause the completion check to use an inconsistent value mid-subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with throttled=false and no limit, where isComplete() only returns true when limitHit (which is never true without a limit), causing the unthrottled retriever to poll for exactly maxPolls+1 times and then complete, potentially missing messages that arrive after the last poll?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where the computed startTime (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) overflows a long value if consensusTimestamp is Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the next poll to use Long.MIN_VALUE as startTime and retrieve all messages from the beginning of time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() instance (created once in the constructor) across all subscriptions, where a large number of concurrent subscribers saturate the bounded elastic pool, causing new subscriptions to queue indefinitely and appear hung?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout being applied on the same scheduler used for polling (timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler)), where scheduler saturation delays the timeout signal itself, allowing subscriptions to run beyond the intended timeout duration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with a very high message volume and no limit, causing Flux.fromStream() to wrap a large in-memory List (from getResultList()) that is never garbage collected until the stream is fully consumed, causing sustained heap pressure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions with throttled=true and no limit, where each subscription's PollingContext holds a Stopwatch, AtomicLong, AtomicReference, and other objects, causing significant heap memory consumption proportional to the number of concurrent subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that there is no per-user or per-topic subscription limit enforced in TopicMessageRetriever.retrieve(), allowing a single user to open an unlimited number of concurrent subscriptions to the same topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value just slightly above startTime, causing the query to execute with a very narrow time window that returns 0 results, and in throttled mode isComplete() returns true (pageSize=0 < maxPageSize), completing immediately but consuming a database query per subscription attempt?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff exponential backoff where the base duration is 1 second and there is no maximum backoff cap specified, causing the backoff duration to grow exponentially and potentially overflow Duration arithmetic after many retries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger rapid subscription open/close cycles, causing onComplete() to be called at high frequency and generating excessive log output that fills disk storage or overwhelms the logging infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retriever does not validate the topicId against an access control list, allowing enumeration of all topic IDs by subscribing to sequential numeric topic IDs and observing whether messages are returned?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to exactly Long.MAX_VALUE, causing (int)(Long.MAX_VALUE - 0) to overflow to -1, and Math.min(-1, maxPageSize) to return -1, causing typedQuery.setMaxResults(-1) which in JPA/Hibernate may be interpreted as 'no limit', returning all rows?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of backpressure enforcement between poll() results and the downstream gRPC subscriber, causing the server to buffer large numbers of TopicMessage objects in memory when the client is slow to consume?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with throttled=true and a very large startTime close to the current time, causing the retriever to poll repeatedly (Long.MAX_VALUE times) with each poll returning 0 results, generating Long.MAX_VALUE empty database queries over the subscription lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newFilter is built with filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() without re-validating the new filter through the @Validated constraint, allowing the derived filter to bypass the @Min(0) startTime constraint if startTime overflows?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the METRIC tag 'hiero_mirror_grpc_retriever' is applied to all subscriptions uniformly, preventing operators from distinguishing abusive subscribers from legitimate ones in metrics, enabling sustained abuse without detection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription and keep it alive by never consuming messages (slow consumer), causing the reactive pipeline to buffer messages indefinitely in memory while the subscription remains open until the timeout fires?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)) wraps a JPA Stream that holds an open database cursor/connection until fully consumed, and if the subscriber cancels mid-stream, the underlying Stream may not be closed, causing a database connection leak?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnCancel(context::onComplete) handler to trigger log injection via a crafted subscriberId when cancelling a subscription, since onComplete() logs the subscriberId without sanitization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled isComplete() condition where both pageSize.get() < maxPageSize AND limitHit must be evaluated, and by crafting a limit exactly equal to total.get() at a race condition moment, cause the subscription to complete one message early, resulting in data loss for the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the timeout is a global wall-clock timeout applied to the entire subscription, meaning a subscriber who opens a subscription just before a database outage will consume a full timeout slot even if no data is ever returned, blocking that timeout slot from being reused?
